summary: Matched the sysdolphin/baselib shadow object by replacing the 1309-line assembly fallback with the C implementation, marking shadow.c as Matching in configure.py, and switching obj_files.mk from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c.o. The C changes completed missing shadow/viewing-rect routines and adjusted declarations/includes so the object could build and match.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s, removing the prior 1309-line assembly implementation for the shadow object.
Changed configure.py entry for sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed obj_files.mk to link $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.o.
In shadow.c, added includes for baselib mtx.h, dolphin/mtx/vec.h, and MSL/trigf.h, supporting vector operations and atan2f/fabsf-style math used by the new code.
Changed shadow_alloc_data from an extern declaration to a C definition in shadow.c.
Replaced raw extern assert strings and __assert use in HSD_ShadowInit with HSD_ASSERT(245, shadow).
Added HSD_ShadowSetViewingRect, which updates the shadow camera viewing rectangle differently for perspective, orthographic, and frustum projections.
Added HSD_ViewingRectInit to initialize origin, eye/right/up vectors, distance, bounds, and perspective mode for HSD_ViewingRect.
Added HSD_ViewingRectAddRect to project/add a rectangle into accumulated viewing bounds, handling perspective scaling and ignoring points behind the eye for perspective mode.

In shadow.h, changed HSD_ViewingRect.perspective from f32 to int and added prototypes for several shadow APIs, including HSD_ShadowAddObject, HSD_ShadowStartRender, HSD_ShadowEndRender, HSD_ShadowSetActive, HSD_ShadowSetSize, HSD_ShadowSetViewingRect, HSD_ViewingRectInit, and HSD_ViewingRectAddRect.
smart_moves: The PR treated matching as an object-level cleanup: after completing/adjusting C, it removed the asm file and updated both configure.py and obj_files.mk so the build actually used the matching C object.
Using HSD_ASSERT instead of file-local extern assert string symbols reduced hand-managed string declarations while preserving the assert line evidence needed for matching.
The new HSD_ASSERT2 macro preserved a custom assertion text path where plain HSD_ASSERT was not sufficient, which is a useful compromise when exact assert string emission matters.

The viewing-rect implementation uses existing Dolphin vector helpers such as VECSubtract, VECNormalize, VECCrossProduct, VECDotProduct, VECScale, and VECMag rather than open-coding vector math, improving type alignment with SDK/baselib conventions.

Projection-specific branching in HSD_ShadowSetViewingRect mirrors the CObj abstraction: perspective updates aspect/FOV, ortho uses HSD_CObjSetOrtho, and frustum scales by near/distance before HSD_CObjSetFrustum.
Changing HSD_ViewingRect.perspective to int aligns the struct field with how the new functions consume it as a boolean/control flag, avoiding a suspicious float flag.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib object is ready to match, update all three places together: delete the asm fallback, mark the source Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py, and switch obj_files.mk from asm .s.o to src .c.o.

Assert macro selection can be matching-critical: prefer project macros like HSD_ASSERT when they generate the right file/line/string pattern, but keep a local macro if a custom assertion text argument is required.
Header struct field types can block matching; even a boolean-like field declared as f32 instead of int should be revisited when reconstructing routines that branch on it.
Completing an object may require adding prototypes for functions that already exist in the C file or are now externally visible, not just writing the missing function bodies.
For HSD camera/shadow code, reuse HSD_CObj accessors and setters instead of directly poking projection fields unless the assembly proves direct access.
For GameCube vector math, using SDK-style VEC* functions can help recover expected call structure and avoid arithmetic ordering differences.
naming_conventions: Public HSD shadow APIs use the HSD_Shadow* prefix, for example HSD_ShadowSetViewingRect, HSD_ShadowStartRender, HSD_ShadowEndRender, and HSD_ShadowSetActive.
Viewing rectangle helpers use the HSD_ViewingRect* prefix, for example HSD_ViewingRectInit, HSD_ViewingRectCheck, and HSD_ViewingRectAddRect.
Local assert string variables in the added area follow descriptive lower-camel names with Assert suffix, such as radiusAssert and aAssert; distAssert is referenced by HSD_ASSERT2 in the diff.
Projection enum cases used in the switch are PROJ_PERSPECTIVE, PROJ_ORTHO, and PROJ_FRUSTUM.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the shadow.s assembly once the C source matched, rather than keeping a mixed C/asm object.
Converted the build object path from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c.o in obj_files.mk.
Marked sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py.
Used explicit floating constants such as 0.0F and FLT_MAX defined as 3.4028235E38F in the added C, likely to control exact constant emission.
Used atan2f and fabsf_bitwise in the perspective viewing-rect path, preserving single-precision/math-library behavior visible in matching-sensitive code.

Kept assert line numbers explicit, including HSD_ASSERT(721, shadow), HSD_ASSERT2(725, distAssert, distance > 0.0F), HSD_ASSERT(754, scale > 0.0F), HSD_ASSERT(762, 0), HSD_ASSERT(795, rect), HSD_ASSERT(855, rect), and HSD_ASSERT(856, position).
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so there is no human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Check nearby shadow.c history for later cleanup of HSD_ASSERT2 or assert string variables such as distAssert/radiusAssert/aAssert.
Search for other baselib objects that still link asm .s.o in obj_files.mk while configure.py has a source file available.
Compare HSD_ViewingRect struct usage in downstream PRs to confirm the int perspective field remained stable.
Look for other HSD_CObj projection routines where PROJ_PERSPECTIVE/PROJ_ORTHO/PROJ_FRUSTUM switch patterns helped matching.
